key_insight: FormalAnalyticGeo turns analytic geometry problem generation into a closed-loop neural-symbolic pipeline by using a formal intermediate language (CDL) to guarantee diagram-text consistency and automatic answer extraction.
engineering_relevance: This is a scalable data-engineering framework for creating high-quality multimodal math datasets, reducing manual annotation while improving geometric precision for training and evaluating MLLMs.
method: Generate analytic geometry problems with an LLM
Formalize each problem into CDL for SDF-based diagram rendering
Verify and retry outputs in a quality-control loop, then measure ground-truth answers from rendered diagrams
evidence_signal: The resulting AnalyticGeo7K dataset has over 7K verified multimodal problems, with a median ground-truth relative error of 0.70% and 82.3% of answers within 5% of the symbolic solution.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on accurate formalization and vision-based measurement; errors in CDL conversion or rendering can still propagate into the generated labels.
paper_title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
